Title: Creating a category
Summary: Pick an id, a display name, a color, and a property schema. Then start filling it.
Body:
## The flow

From [Game codex](/codex), click "+ New category". The form asks for:

- **Category id**: lowercase slug, used as the folder name on disk. Locked once saved.
- **Display name**: human readable label shown in headers.
- **Color**: one of eight palette colors. Tints the section header on the list page and the card stripe on each entry.
- **Properties**: an array of property defs. Each carries a Key, Label, Type, Required flag, and (for refs) a RefDomain.

Save and you land on the category's entry list, ready to add entries.

## Property schema

The schema is the contract. Every entry in a category has the three default fields (Image, Description, Path) plus whatever properties you defined. The entry form auto generates controls based on each property's Type. See [property types](/help/codex/property-types) for what each one does.

## Sorting properties

The schema editor uses up and down arrows on each property row. There is no drag and drop reorder; the arrows scale better when there are 10+ properties and avoid the precision issues of dragging on tablets.

## Validation

Properties go through two validation passes when an entry is saved:

1. Type vs value: a number property cannot hold a string, a tags property must be an array of strings, a required property cannot be empty.
2. FK existence (client only, layered on top): a ref property whose target id does not exist in the catalog flags as a warning in the form and as an error on the [Integrity tab](/diagnostics/integrity).
